text: Sunday Thoughts on Smarter We₿ Disclaimer: Longer post ahead. TL;DR — Fundamentals remain strong. Ignore short-term noise. Execution getting stronger each week. Growth potential is enormous. Last week was a mix of emotions — on one hand, we achieved big milestones: ✅ Largest capital raise to date ✅ Largest single BTC purchase ✅ Most significant strategic meeting yet (Michael Saylor) ✅ Notable direct investment secured And yet, paradoxically, the share price moved in the opposite direction. It’s important to recognise that a week like this was inevitable, especially after the outsized post-IPO rally. Ironically, had there been no news, the price action would be easier to accept as “profit-taking after a strong run.” But markets aren’t always that efficient or rational. Having spent 20+ years on equities trading floors, I’ve seen this dynamic play out time and time again. Investors ask, “What’s happening with Stock X?” — when in reality, markets often disconnect from fundamentals, both to the upside and downside. Price isn’t always an immediate reflection of progress and that’s critical to remember. History offers countless examples: Every major growth story from Amazon to Bitcoin itself has experienced sharp, prolonged drawdowns. Sometimes driven by news; other times, simply a function of rotation or profit-taking. These periods test conviction, but are normal in the long term, upward cycle of growth stocks. As frustrating (and sometimes painful) as they are, these moments are necessary — just as challenges help strengthen character in life, they build resilience across a company, its community, and its stakeholders. What really stood out to me this week was the response from both the $SWC community and management. Honestly, it’s the most proud I’ve felt to be a shareholder. It’s easy to show up when things are in “up only” mode — but moments like last week reveal our true character. The overwhelming majority of online posts were constructive, with thoughtful content shared to help inform, reassure, and maintain perspective. We’re now a community of 2,800+ — thank you to everyone contributing every like and post goes a long way to helping build awareness. Long term HODLers of #SWC understand the fundamentals, the strategy, and the clear roadmap for execution. @asjwebley ’s visibility and leadership have been exceptional — 10/10. He’s shown up every week since IPO, actively engaging with the community, buying stock, and, most recently, delivering a calm, reassuring interview on Friday with @ourgoodlifeuk . His consistency, credibility, and commitment to transparency are exactly why I believe $SWC will become the dominant player in the UK. It must take a lot of his time being on X but it’s inspiring to watch, and it motivates all of us to raise our own standards — I know it does for me. As for what next week brings in terms of price? Who knows. But what I do know is this: There’s a clear plan. The team is executing every week. Confidence, both internal and external, is growing. And both top down and bottom up factors make me increasingly bullish on $SWC . I know it’s hard, but try to ignore the short term noise. Focus on the mission. The opportunity ahead is massive.

text: Smarter Web ($SWC): Episode 8 Summary - BULLISH! See my interpretation from today’s episode which couldn’t have come at a better time. Remember, despite the toughest week for the share price YTD, the company had it's largest week ever (biggest raise, biggest purchase, meeting with Saylor and a single strategic investor etc). I don’t have a crystal ball, but think if you are holding this for the long term you will “be doing ok”. Meeting with Michael Saylor • Andrew met with Michael Saylor yesterday in London; they spent over an hour together. • Saylor is highly supportive of SWC’s current capital raise strategy via pure equity. • He believes SWC is doing an excellent job and could become the dominant Bitcoin treasury company in the UK. • Saylor sees potential for SWC to exceed Andrew’s current vision, including ambitions around FTSE inclusion and scale. • Offered his availability for ongoing support and is happy to answer questions anytime. • Andrew was touched by Saylor’s time and shared some Smarter Web merchandise (t-shirts) with him. Company Position & Market View • Company is stronger than ever, with over £100 million accumulated on the balance sheet within just two months. • Andrew believes the current share price offers good relative value; this view is supported by his recent personal share purchase. • He attributes some recent price action to market rotation into newer BTC vehicles. Strategic Vision & Future Targets • Andrew received another inbound message from a well-respected industry figure requesting a meeting. • Andrew and Jesse have been discussing SWC’s next BTC target; the number is seen as achievable with a clear, defined roadmap. • Andrew gave some clues and my personal opinion is that the target may be 2,100 BTC (0.01% of the Bitcoin network), which they expect to reach relatively quickly. Investor Relations & Education • A new investor deck is being prepared for the website, aimed at prospective shareholders. • UK institutions still require education on the “Bitcoin treasury company” model, which remains better understood by retail investors. • No negative feedback from investors regarding recent share price movements post-bookbuild; most are focused on long-term growth. • The recent raise was a mix of UK and international investors, including both institutions and high-net-worth individuals, existing and new participants. • Andrew feels fortunate to have strong investor support. Capital Raise Considerations • Reiterated with the subscription agreement that they cannot sell stock below the previous day’s price (“no down-day sales”). • Emphasised the balance required between selling for capital needs while allowing the share price to breathe. • Open to one-off subscriptions if deemed in the company’s best interest. • Convertible bonds have been reviewed but are not considered optimal; no short-term plans to pursue them. • Pure equity provides greater control; other debt instruments unlikely unless terms are exceptionally favourable. • Andrew stressed he would never pursue actions detrimental to common shareholders. • Commission on bookbuilds ranges from 0–5%; subscription agreement fees 3%. Market Dynamics & Positioning • Some speculation around short activity, though borrow remains difficult to source. • Positive sentiment around Aquis as a platform for raising capital; seen as more flexible than the LSE, which has certain limitations. • Absence of WRAP doesn’t prevent retail from supporting SWC via open-market purchases. Industry Landscape & Peer Activity • Andrew acknowledged growing competition in the BTC treasury space but believes: • Space exists for a few smaller, responsible participants but there will ultimately just be one, dominant player in each region. • Simon from Metaplanet advised ignoring short-term noise from emerging peers and staying focused on SWC’s strategy. • Andrew reinforced their laser-focus on executing the core plan.

text: Thoughts on Today’s Price Action — $SWC What a day. The London line closed down 14% at 412.5p (vs. a 480p prior close). Intraday we hit a low of 193p (-60%) followed by a sharp 113% rally off the lows. Despite closing lower, I believe today will be seen as a constructive moment in $SWC’s journey — and here’s why: Firstly, from multiple conversations, consensus is that today’s aggressive sell off was largely flow driven, not news driven. $COIN started rolling over at a similar time driven by one or more sellers who underestimated the impact they would. Clunky trading platforms struggled to quote efficiently, adding to the volatility. But critically, we saw strong buyers step in on weakness, with accumulation continuing all the way into the London close - that’s extremely constructive for sentiment. We’ve now seen multiple 30%+ drawdowns — some intraday, some over several days — but each time, buyers have come in aggressively. When a CEO can say, “Our stock is volatile, but when it’s down, people want to buy that dip,” that’s a powerful signal. I appreciate today’s price swings were uncomfortable, especially for those unfamiliar with this level of volatility. But that’s part of $SWC’s DNA — and part of what makes this story so unique. UK markets have been starved of high-beta, high-volatility names for years, dominated by defensive sectors like staples, pharma, and tobacco. Over time, this type of price action builds a foundation for instruments like convertible bonds, where demand is heavily dependent on liquidity and volatility (CB desks want vol). Finally we also set a new record for volume traded on the London line today, another encouraging sign of engagement in the stock. Looking forward to seeing how the US line trades, but the key takeaway for me is clear — pullbacks are healthy, sharp drawdowns continue to attract buyers, we’ve announced our largest Bitcoin purchase to date, and the fundamentals remain solid.

text: $SWC : Updated thoughts The more I comprehend and model today’s announcement, the more undeniably bullish I am on $SWC . It’s honestly hard to overstate just how transformative this Subscription Agreement could be for the company. One of the main pushbacks on $SWC’s strategy had been whether they could consistently raise capital at progressively higher prices, especially from institutional investors. This new mechanism removes a huge amount of pressure on that front. While I expect there will still be follow-on raises as institutional interest grows, the control, flexibility, and efficiency this structure provides is a total game-changer. The 21m share Subscription Agreement gives me serious déjà vu of when Strategy ($MSTR) launched its 21/21 Plan in Q4 2024. They announced a $21B ATM equity program through 2027 — not only did the stock rise the next day (despite the dilution), they ended up using the full allocation within six months, then followed it with the even larger 42/42 Plan. $SWC is currently up 17% despite an implied 9.5% dilution (21m/220m shs outstanding). Looking at where $SWC’s mNAV premium is today, and the opportunity for hugely accretive dilution (i.e. selling shares well above NAV to buy more Bitcoin and increase Bitcoin per Share), I genuinely believe they could place the entire 21m shares within a very quick time frame and I would not be surprised to see them reload this facility when complete (as MSTR did with the 42/42 plan). Note the daily volume cap of 20% shouldn't be a constraint given the recent uptick in liquidity on the London line. I’m still working through the AQSE rules on whether there's a formal limit on share issuance (as a % of total outstanding) without a shareholder vote, but even if one were required, it’s reasonable to assume it would pass — the shareholder base is highly aligned and strategically motivated. Bottom line: this is a massive unlock for $SWC . The ATM-like function enhances their ability to scale their Bitcoin treasury without the friction of traditional capital raises, and it aligns perfectly with the playbook used by the most successful public Bitcoin treasuries globally (Strategy and Metaplanet). It’s genuinely hard to quantify just how bullish this is.

text: You're correct there is no short selling on the Aquis Access tier where $SWC is listed. But it’s worth noting that shorting isn’t as straightforward as it sounds. To short a stock, there needs to be an available / liquid borrow market, which has to be arranged through a broker. This borrow often comes at an annualized fee, which can be very high for volatile or illiquid stocks — we’ve seen this with names like MSTR. That said, short interest can actually be a positive. It adds liquidity, creates two-way flow, and helps price discovery. Michael Saylor has openly welcomed short sellers in MSTR for exactly that reason — he wants it to be a liquid vehicle for expressing views on Bitcoin, long or short. In the end, it’s the market that decides who’s right over different time horizons.

text: $SWC group has now passed 1,700 members, and it’s great to see more international community members joining, welcome. One question that keeps coming up is: what's the key difference between $SWC (UK) and $ALTBG (Blockchain Group in France) as both are geographically based in Europe. It’s important to note that the UK left the European Union in 2020 (Brexit) and now operates under its own regulatory framework - thus, the most important distinction is how retail have accessed Bitcoin exposure. Before the launch of $SWC (and Coinsilium), UK retail investors had no practical way to gain listed Bitcoin exposure. Only institutions or certified “professional investors” could access Bitcoin ETFs, meaning most UK retail investors were had to use Strategy ($MSTR) as a proxy. With the arrival of $SWC , UK retail investors now have direct access to a listed vehicle for Bitcoin exposure and crucially, one that can be held within a tax-efficient ISA wrapper. There has been significant pent-up demand in the UK market for years, which is now flowing into $SWC , with many now reallocating from $MSTR - all that locked capital and energy now has a place to go that's on home soil. Note there are discussions for UK retail to have access to an ETN but no time line has been set By contrast, retail investors in Europe and France (where ALTBG is listed) have had some access to regulated Bitcoin ETPs and ETFs since at least 2021, with full access by 2022/2023. So while both companies are geographically based in Europe, there is a structural, regulatory and cultural difference that affects how quickly how much retail capital can flow into each stock. This is a key factor to consider when comparing traction and demand across both.
